tluaLengthOperator.tlua(18,13): error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
tluaLengthOperator.tlua(19,13): error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
tluaLengthOperator.tlua(21,13): error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
tluaLengthOperator.tlua(34,13): error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
tluaLengthOperator.tlua(38,13): error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.


==== tluaLengthOperator.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // Lua's `#` is a unary length operator: a string or a table yields a number.
    
    local ls: number = #"abc";
    
    // `#` composes like any prefix operator.
    local sum: number = #"ab" + #"cd";
    
    // A table works when its type carries a numeric index: an array type or an explicit index
    // signature both qualify.
    function lenArr(arr: number[]): number
      return #arr;
    end
    function lenIndex(t: { [i: number]: number }): number
      return #t;
    end
    
    // Errors: a number, a boolean, or a function has no length.
    local e1 = #5;
                ~
!!! error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
    local e2 = #true;
                ~~~~
!!! error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
    function fn(): void end
    local e3 = #fn;
                ~~
!!! error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
    
    // A record with no numeric index measures too: Lua returns a border (0 for no
    // array part) for ANY table, so only non-tables reject.
    local rec = { x = 1 };
    local recLen: number = #rec;
    
    // A union measures when every arm does; a non-table arm rejects the whole.
    declare arrOrRec: number[] | { x: number };
    local unionLen: number = #arrOrRec;
    declare strOrRec: string | { x: number };
    local mixedLen: number = #strOrRec;
    declare numOrRec: number | { x: number };
    local e4 = #numOrRec;
                ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
    
    // Opaque handles are not tables: `#` on a thread or userdata errors at runtime.
    declare th: LuaThread;
    local e5 = #th;
                ~~
!!! error TLUA100042: The '#' operator can only be applied to a string or a table.
